# Session — 2026-05-02

## Commits

### 15:53 — Narad [`962c59e`]
**feat: Phase 0a — Narad routing accuracy spike**

Sets up the test harness to validate whether Gemma 4 E4B can route user
tasks to the correct avatar(s) with reliable structured output.

Components:
- narad_schema.py: Pydantic routing schema (NaradRouting) with token-level
  grammar enforcement via outlines. Invalid JSON is impossible to emit.
- narad_claude.py: Claude Sonnet baseline evaluator using tool-use API for
  guaranteed structured output. Target: ≥95% weighted accuracy.
- narad_local.py: mlx-lm + outlines evaluator for local Gemma 4B on Apple
  Silicon. Target: ≥80% weighted accuracy (escalate if <70%).
- test_prompts.json: 50 ground-truth labelled prompts across all 7 avatars,
  multi-avatar scenarios, and edge cases.
- run_evaluation.py: CLI harness with per-category breakdown, go/no-go
  verdict, and Claude-vs-local comparison report.

Definition of done: zero parse failures (outlines enforces schema), local
accuracy ≥80% or escalate to 27B MoE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```

```

### 16:12 — Narad [`021fef5`]
**feat: add GPT-4o baseline evaluator**

narad_openai.py uses client.beta.chat.completions.parse() with the
NaradRouting Pydantic model — same structured output guarantee as the
Claude tool-use path but uses OPENAI_API_KEY instead.

run_evaluation.py now accepts --model gpt, --model claude, or --model local.
compare_results() auto-detects whichever baseline file exists (gpt or claude).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
A	phase-0a/narad_openai.py
M	phase-0a/requirements.txt
M	phase-0a/run_evaluation.py
```

### 16:38 — Narad [`8dfe06f`]
**fix: outlines 1.x mlxlm API + tighten Rama/Krishna/Buddha boundaries**

narad_local.py: from_mlxlm(model, tokenizer) replaces om.mlxlm(model_id)
which was renamed in outlines 1.x.

narad_schema.py: GPT-4o baseline revealed Rama/Krishna boundary leak on
every SOP task and Buddha/Krishna confusion on evaluation tasks. Tightened
system prompt descriptions to clarify:
- Rama owns all structured-sequence outputs (no Krishna just for readability)
- Krishna is specifically for persuasive/stakeholder prose
- Buddha is specifically for analytical judgement, not general review
- Parashurama handles full implementation without forced Rama/Buddha additions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
M	phase-0a/narad_local.py
M	phase-0a/narad_schema.py
```
Stats:  2 files changed, 9 insertions(+), 9 deletions(-)

### 16:50 — Narad [`46c3433`]
**fix: outlines 1.x Generator API for mlx-lm**

Replace deprecated outlines.generate.json() with outlines.Generator(model, output_type).
Load pattern: from_mlxlm(mlx_model, tokenizer) → outlines.Generator(model, NaradRouting).
Verified: SteerableGenerator builds cleanly, model loads from cache.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

```
M	phase-0a/narad_local.py
```

### 17:21 — Narad [`0a9bfdd`]
**fix: parse string output from outlines SteerableGenerator into NaradRouting**

outlines 1.x SteerableGenerator returns a JSON string, not a Pydantic
instance. Added isinstance branching: str->model_validate_json,
dict->model_validate, else pass through.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

```
M	phase-0a/narad_local.py
```

### 17:42 — Narad [`5acdf68`]
**fix(prompt): surgical fixes for Narasimha/Matsya/Parashurama routing**

- Narasimha: added explicit trigger words (bug/error/crash/stuck/OOMKilled)
  and hard rule: do NOT use Buddha or Matsya for debugging tasks
- Matsya: restricted to live external search only; explicit ban on using
  it for examples/best-practices the model already knows
- Varaha: clarified it only reads documents the USER has attached
- Parashurama: expanded to cover code review, API conversion, security audits
- Added rule 4: ban on Matsya as a 'find examples' helper

Root cause: 6/9 misses were Narasimha; model was routing bugs to
Buddha+Matsya instead. Matsya over-routing drove ~30 partial matches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
A	phase-0a/narad_schema.py
```
Stats:  1 file changed, 114 insertions(+)

### 18:24 — Narad [`24d476f`]
**feat: Phase 0b — Narad ADK supervisor + 7 avatar tools + SSE server**

- avatar_tools.py: 7 FunctionTool stubs; docstrings are the routing signal GPT-4o uses
- narad_agent.py: LlmAgent(GPT-4o via LiteLlm) with all 7 tools
- server.py: FastAPI SSE endpoint with locked event taxonomy (avatar_start, avatar_done, narad_synthesis, done)
- run_demo.py: CLI end-to-end demo (no server needed)

Option C confirmed: GPT-4o as Narad runtime. Local routing revisited in Phase 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### 22:57 — Narad [`f04f138`]
**fix: replace AgentTool with FunctionTool wrappers for LiteLlm compat**

AgentTool serialises invocations as text instead of executing them
when paired with LiteLlm in ADK 1.32. FunctionTool wrappers that
internally run each LlmAgent via a mini-runner restore the working
invoke_* interface proven in Phase 0b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
M	phase-1/avatar_agents.py
M	phase-1/server.py
```
Stats:  2 files changed, 59 insertions(+), 23 deletions(-)

### 23:15 — Narad [`bc610e2`]
**feat: Phase 2a — Smriti memory layer (LanceDB + OpenAI embeddings)**

Per-user semantic memory: recall() injects relevant past avatar outputs
into each task; remember() stores results after each avatar completes.
Scoped by user_id across sessions. Best-effort (never blocks main flow).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### 23:25 — Narad [`6d666af`]
**feat: Phase 2b/c — Matsya live search (Tavily) + Yantra observability**

Matsya: FunctionTool wrapping Tavily web_search, degrades gracefully
without TAVILY_API_KEY. Prompt updated to always call search first.

Yantra: JSONL trace per session in phase-2/yantra_traces/. Captures
avatar_start/done with latency_ms, session total_ms. Exposed via
GET /trace/{session_id} on the FastAPI server.

Smriti: re-implemented directly on LanceDB (mem0 2.x dropped lancedb
support). OpenAI text-embedding-3-small for vectors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
M	phase-1/avatar_agents.py
M	phase-1/server.py
A	phase-2/matsya_search.py
M	phase-2/requirements.txt
A	phase-2/yantra.py
A	phase-2/yantra_traces/6674e095-57e3-4cf9-b343-663768dd4595.jsonl
```
Stats:  6 files changed, 239 insertions(+), 17 deletions(-)

### 23:54 — Narad [`1f8556c`]
**fix: replace Phase 0a JSON prompt with ADK-native instruction for Narad**

NARAD_SYSTEM_PROMPT ended with \"Return ONLY a JSON object\" which bled
into the synthesis step, causing Narad to output routing JSON instead of
prose. New instruction preserves avatar routing rules but explicitly
directs Narad to synthesise in plain English after tool calls complete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Files changed:
```
M	phase-1/narad_agent.py
```
Stats:  1 file changed, 36 insertions(+), 9 deletions(-)
